config.title: Prenzlauer Berg Berlin - FAQ
config.subtitle: Quick answers on where Prenzlauer Berg is, how to get there, and what is actually worth your time in the district.

items.q: Where is Prenzlauer Berg and how do I get there?
items.a: Prenzlauer Berg sits just north of Berlin's centre, in the former East, about ten minutes from Alexanderplatz. The easiest way in is the U2, which stops at Senefelderplatz, Eberswalder Straße and Schönhauser Allee along the district's spine, and several trams serve it too. It is well inside the city, so a normal AB ticket covers the journey. Aim for Eberswalder Straße as your entry point, since it drops you in the middle of the best part.

items.q: What is there to do in Prenzlauer Berg?
items.a: The appeal is the streets themselves rather than one big sight. A good half-day loop is: currywurst at Konnopke's under the Eberswalder Straße viaduct, the free Museum in der Kulturbrauerei on East German life, the weekly market on Kollwitzplatz, the Wasserturm on its little park, a coffee and beer garden on Kastanienallee, and, on a Sunday, the Mauerpark flea market and karaoke.

items.q: Is the Museum in der Kulturbrauerei free?
items.a: Yes. The Museum in der Kulturbrauerei, inside the old brewery complex, is free to enter and open Tuesday to Sunday. Its permanent exhibition covers everyday life in the GDR through around 800 real objects, including a Trabant with a roof tent and a complete newspaper kiosk. Give it about half an hour.

The weekly market on Kollwitzplatz runs on Thursdays and Saturdays, with more than fifty stalls of Brandenburg produce, cheese, bread and cooked food, plus benches to sit and eat. It is the best time to visit the square. Buy lunch from a stall and eat it on a bench for the real Prenzlauer Berg experience.

items.q: Is Konnopke's Imbiss open on Sunday?
items.a: No. Konnopke's Imbiss, the currywurst stand under the Eberswalder Straße railway bridge that has served this corner since 1930, is open Monday to Saturday and closed on Sundays. If you visit on a Sunday for the market and Mauerpark, plan your currywurst for another meal or use one of the other nearby stands.

items.q: What is the best day to visit Prenzlauer Berg?
items.a: It depends on what you want. Sunday is the liveliest day, with the Mauerpark flea market and afternoon Bearpit Karaoke, but note that Konnopke's is closed on Sundays. Thursday or Saturday gives you the Kollwitzplatz market instead. The Museum in der Kulturbrauerei is open Tuesday to Sunday. For a first visit, a Sunday captures the district's famous weekend energy best.
